message.content:
Can you websearch for more recent information than below page concerning Fable outage?


https://www.anthropic.com/news/fable-mythos-access

addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- claude-code-guide: Use this agent when the user asks questions ("Can Claude...", "Does Claude...", "How do I...") about: (1) Claude Code (the CLI tool) - features, hooks, slash commands, MCP servers, settings, IDE integrations, keyboard shortcuts; (2) Claude Agent SDK - building custom agents; (3) Claude API (formerly Anthropic API) - API usage, tool use, Anthropic SDK usage. **IMPORTANT:** Before spawning a new agent, check if there is already a running or recently completed claude-code-guide agent that you can continue via SendMessage. (Tools: Bash, Read, WebFetch, WebSearch)

- Explore: Read-only search agent for broad fan-out searches — when answering means sweeping many files, directories, or naming conventions and you only need the conclusion, not the file dumps. It reads excerpts rather than whole files, so it locates code; it doesn't review or audit it. Specify search breadth: "medium" for moderate exploration, "very thorough" for multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

addedBlocks:
## vvx
Job Jockey Kit - MCP tools for project initiative management

attachment.content:
- cma-prep-pr: Prepare branch for upstream PR contribution
- rbk-prep-release: Prepare release candidate for upstream delivery
- rbk-wrangle: Muster, then roster RBK and JJK heats as two space-padded plain-text blocks
- vvc-BREAK-LOCK: Force-break a stuck VVC lock after a crash.
- vvc-commit: Guarded git commit
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

aiTitle: Search for recent Fable outage information

content.text: I'll search for recent information about a Fable outage. Let me load the web tools first.
Let me first read the referenced page to understand the baseline, then search for newer information.

input.prompt: Summarize this page. What does it say about a Fable outage or access issue? Include any dates, timelines, status, and affected services mentioned.

input.query: Anthropic Fable outage access status update

content.content:
# Summary: Fable 5 and Mythos 5 Access Suspension

**Date & Timeline:**
On June 12, 2026, at 5:21pm ET, Anthropic received a US government directive requiring immediate suspension of Fable 5 and Mythos 5 access.

**What Happened:**
The government invoked national security authorities to issue an export control order. The directive applies to all users globally, including foreign nationals and Anthropic employees. The company states: "The net effect of this order is that we must abruptly disable Fable 5 and Mythos 5 for **all** our customers to ensure compliance."

**Reason Given:**
The government believed it had identified a jailbreak method bypassing model safeguards. Anthropic reviewed the demonstration and found it involved "asking the model to read a specific codebase and fix any software flaws"—capabilities Anthropic says are already available in competing models and used routinely by cybersecurity professionals.

**Status:**
The models remain disabled. Anthropic is complying with the legal directive while disagreeing with the decision and working to restore access.

**Other Services:**
Access to all other Anthropic models remains unaffected.

# Summary: Fable 5 and Mythos 5 Access Suspension

**Date & Timeline:**
On June 12, 2026, at 5:21pm ET, Anthropic received a US government directive requiring immediate suspension of Fable 5 and Mythos 5 access.

**What Happened:**
The government invoked national security authorities to issue an export control order. The directive applies to all users globally, including foreign nationals and Anthropic employees. The company states: "The net effect of this order is that we must abruptly disable Fable 5 and Mythos 5 for **all** our customers to ensure compliance."

**Reason Given:**
The government believed it had identified a jailbreak method bypassing model safeguards. Anthropic reviewed the demonstration and found it involved "asking the model to read a specific codebase and fix any software flaws"—capabilities Anthropic says are already available in competing models and used routinely by cybersecurity professionals.

**Status:**
The models remain disabled. Anthropic is complying with the legal directive while disagreeing with the decision and working to restore access.

**Other Services:**
Access to all other Anthropic models remains unaffected.

Links: [{"title":"Anthropic Suspend Access to Fable 5 and Mythos 5 After US Government Export Directive","url":"https://beincrypto.com/anthropic-suspends-fable-5-mythos-5-us-directive/"},{"title":"Claude Status","url":"https://status.claude.com/"},{"title":"Anthropic suspends new AI models after government directive","url":"https://www.nbcnews.com/tech/tech-news/anthropic-suspends-new-ai-models-fable-mythos-government-directive-rcna349901"},{"title":"Anthropic Halts Access to Fable 5 and Mythos 5 - ServeTheHome","url":"https://www.servethehome.com/anthropic-halts-access-to-fable-5-and-mythos-5/"},{"title":"Anthropic Suspend Access to Fable 5 and Mythos 5 After US Government Export Directive","url":"https://www.yahoo.com/news/politics/articles/anthropic-suspend-access-fable-5-010806634.html"},{"title":"Anthropic Disables Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 After US Government Order - MarkTechPost","url":"https://www.marktechpost.com/2026/06/13/anthropic-disables-claude-fable-5-and-mythos-5-after-us-government-order/"},{"title":"Anthropic blocks all public access to Claude Fable 5, Mythos 5 following US government order — what enterprises should do | VentureBeat","url":"https://venturebeat.com/technology/anthropic-blocks-all-public-access-to-claude-fable-5-mythos-5-following-us-government-order-what-enterprises-should-do"},{"title":"Anthropic blocks all customers' access to Fable 5 and Mythos 5 - Engadget","url":"https://www.engadget.com/2193656/anthropic-blocks-access-fable-5-mythos-5/"},{"title":"Statement on the US government directive to suspend access to Fable 5 and Mythos 5 \\ Anthropic","url":"https://www.anthropic.com/news/fable-mythos-access"}]

Based on the search results, here's the current status of the Anthropic Fable outage:

## Outage Summary

Anthropic disabled access to Fable 5 and Mythos 5 on June 12 after the US government issued an export control directive citing national security authorities. Access to all other Anthropic models, including the Claude family, remains completely unaffected and continues to operate normally for every customer worldwide.

## Reason for the Suspension

The US government, citing national security authorities, has issued an export control directive to suspend all access to Fable 5 and Mythos 5 by any foreign national, whether inside or outside the United States, including foreign national Anthropic employees. The government believes it has become aware of a method of bypassing, or "jailbreaking" Fable 5.

## Anthropic's Response

The net effect of this order is that we must abruptly disable Fable 5 and Mythos 5 for all our customers to ensure compliance. Anthropic says in a blog post that "We believe this is a misunderstanding and are working to restore access as soon as possible," and apologizes to its customers.

The suspension affects all users globally and represents an unprecedented action by the US government to take a publicly deployed AI model offline.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

Links: [{"title":"Anthropic Claude Fable 5 on AWS: Mythos-class capabilities with built-in safeguards now available | Amazon Web Services","url":"https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/aws/anthropic-claude-fable-5-on-aws-mythos-class-capabilities-with-built-in-safeguards-now-available/"},{"title":"When will Anthropic restore Fable 5 access for US customer | Octagon","url":"https://octagonai.co/markets/politics/when-will-anthropic-restore-fable-5-access-for-us-customers/"},{"title":"Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 is a version of Mythos the public can access today | TechCrunch","url":"https://techcrunch.com/2026/06/09/anthropics-claude-fable-5-is-a-version-of-mythos-the-public-can-access-today/"},{"title":"Claude Fable 5 and Claude Mythos 5 \\ Anthropic","url":"https://www.anthropic.com/news/claude-fable-5-mythos-5"},{"title":"Introducing Claude Fable 5 and Claude Mythos 5 - Claude API Docs","url":"https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/about-claude/models/introducing-claude-fable-5-and-claude-mythos-5"},{"title":"Anthropic Suspended Fable 5 and Mythos 5 Access: The AI DevOps Response | Clanker Cloud Blog","url":"https://clankercloud.ai/blog/anthropic-fable-mythos-suspension-ai-devops-response"},{"title":"ANTHROPIC Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 | by Tahir | Jun, 2026 | Medium","url":"https://medium.com/@tahirbalarabe2/anthropic-claude-fable-5-and-mythos-5-d9012bc1cc0c"},{"title":"Anthropic brings Mythos to the masses with Claude Fable 5, its most powerful generally available model ever | VentureBeat","url":"https://venturebeat.com/technology/anthropic-brings-mythos-to-the-masses-with-claude-fable-5-its-most-powerful-generally-available-model-ever"}]

Based on the search results, here's what I found about the Anthropic Fable 5 and Mythos 5 access situation:

## Summary of Events

As of June 14, 2026, Anthropic suspended global access to both Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 for all customers, including those in the United States. This action followed a US government export control directive issued on June 12, 2026.

## Context

Fable 5 had only recently launched on June 9, 2026, and was initially available to the public via the Claude API and various enterprise plans before its suspension. Claude Fable 5 makes Mythos-level capabilities available to customers, with strong safeguards designed to make it safe for broader use.

## Reason for Suspension

The US government issued an export control directive requiring Anthropic to suspend access to Fable 5 and Mythos 5 by foreign nationals, including foreign national employees, whether inside or outside the United States. The government did not provide specific details of the national security concern, but Anthropic understands the concern to involve a narrow method of bypassing Fable safeguards.

## Current Status

Anthropic is suspending access to Claude Fable 5 and Claude Mythos 5, and apologizes for this disruption to customers while working to restore access as soon as possible. However, there is currently no official timeline for when Anthropic will restore Fable 5 access.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

The net effect of\" / X","url":"https://x.com/AnthropicAI/status/2065597531644743999"},{"title":"Statement on the US government directive to suspend access to Fable 5 and Mythos 5","url":"https://simonwillison.net/2026/Jun/13/us-government-directive-to-suspend-access/"},{"title":"US Gov asks Anthropic to ban 'foreign national' access to Fable, Mythos","url":"https://www.bleepingcomputer.com/news/security/us-gov-asks-anthropic-to-ban-foreign-national-access-to-fable-mythos/"},{"title":"Statement on the US government directive to suspend access to Fable 5 and Mythos 5 \\ Anthropic","url":"https://www.anthropic.com/news/fable-mythos-access"},{"title":"Anthropic suspends new AI models after government directive","url":"https://www.nbcnews.com/tech/tech-news/anthropic-suspends-new-ai-models-fable-mythos-government-directive-rcna349901"},{"title":"When a Government Pulls an AI Model: What the Fable 5 and Mythos 5 Suspension Means for Security Teams | Snyk","url":"https://snyk.io/blog/fable-mythos-suspension-security-takeaways/"},{"title":"Anthropic forced to disable Fable 5 after US directive | Digital Watch Observatory","url":"https://dig.watch/updates/anthropic-fable-5-us-export-control-directive"}]

Here's the latest news on the Fable 5 and Mythos 5 export directive:

## Overview

The US government, citing national security authorities, has issued an export control directive to suspend all access to Fable 5 and Mythos 5 by any foreign national, whether inside or outside the United States, including foreign national Anthropic employees. The net effect of this order is that Anthropic must abruptly disable Fable 5 and Mythos 5 for all customers to ensure compliance.

## Timeline

Anthropic received the directive from the government at 5:21pm (ET) on June 12, 2026, and disabled access to both models for every customer worldwide that same evening.

## Reason for Directive

The government believes it has become aware of a method of bypassing, or "jailbreaking" Fable 5. An administration official told Axios the Commerce Department decided to take the action after another company claimed it was able to jailbreak Mythos, alarming the administration about possible national security risks.

## Anthropic's Response

Anthropic is complying with the government's legal directive and is removing access to Fable 5 and Mythos 5 for all users. However, Anthropic has reviewed a report and validated that the level of capability displayed is widely available from other models (including OpenAI's GPT-5.5), and disagrees that the finding of a narrow potential jailbreak should be cause for recalling a commercial model.

content.text: Let me check the live status page and search for the very latest developments (June 15).

input.query: Anthropic Fable Mythos government directive June 15 2026

input.prompt: What does this say about the Fable 5 / Mythos 5 suspension? Are there any updates after June 13 about restoration, government response, legal challenge, or timeline?

content.content:
# Fable 5 / Mythos 5 Suspension Summary

Based on the webpage content provided, here's what is stated:

## The Suspension

The US government issued an export control directive on June 13, 2026, requiring Anthropic to suspend all access to Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models. The stated reason centered on national security concerns related to a potential jailbreak technique.

## Government's Concern

According to Anthropic's statement, officials believed they had discovered a method for bypassing the models. The technique involved "asking the model to read a specific codebase and fix any software flaws." Anthropic's assessment indicated this capability was "widely available from other models" and represented relatively minor vulnerabilities.

## Access Cutoff

Simon Willison documented that API access was terminated at 6:59pm Pacific time on June 12, 2026, returning a 404 error directing users to Claude Opus 4.8.

## No Updates Provided

The webpage contains **no information about restoration timelines, government responses, legal challenges, or subsequent developments** after June 13. The content consists solely of Anthropic's initial statement and Willison's documentation of when access was disabled.

Based on the search results, here's what happened regarding the Anthropic government directive on June 15, 2026:

Anthropic received a government directive banning all foreign nationals, even ones who work at Anthropic, from accessing Fable 5 and Mythos 5 over national security concerns just three days after publicly launching Fable 5. The company disabled Fable 5 and Mythos 5 for all customers to ensure compliance.

The letter came from US Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick. Anthropic received the directive at 5:21pm (ET).

Regarding the reason for the directive, the US government became concerned that the models might be vulnerable to a "jailbreak" technique that could allow users to discover or exploit vulnerabilities. However, the letter did not state what specifically concerned the government.

Anthropic disputed the reasons behind the restrictions, saying the evidence does not justify such a broad move.

lastPrompt: Can you websearch for more recent information than below page concerning Fable outage?   https://www.anthropic.com/news/fable-mythos-access

aiTitle: Search for recent Fable outage information

content.text:
Here's what's newer than the Anthropic statement page (dated June 12–13). As of today (June 15, 2026), **access has not been restored** — but several details have emerged since that page was published:

## What's new since the original statement

- **Named source of the order:** The directive came from **US Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick**, issued under export-control / national-security authorities. Anthropic received it at 5:21pm ET on June 12 and disabled both models the same evening (API began returning a 404 pointing users to Claude Opus 4.8 at 6:59pm Pacific).
- **What actually triggered it:** Per an administration official speaking to Axios, Commerce acted after **another company claimed it had jailbroken Mythos**, which alarmed the administration. The official letter reportedly did *not* specify the concern in detail.
- **Anthropic's pushback has sharpened:** Anthropic now says it reviewed the demonstration — it amounted to "asking the model to read a specific codebase and fix any software flaws" — and **validated that the same capability is widely available from other models, explicitly naming OpenAI's GPT-5.5.** They call it a misunderstanding and a narrow potential jailbreak that shouldn't justify recalling a commercial model.
- **Scope:** The order bars access by any *foreign national* (inside or outside the US, including Anthropic's own foreign-national employees); to comply, Anthropic disabled the models for **all** customers globally. All other Claude models remain unaffected.

## Current status (June 15)

- **Still disabled. No restoration timeline.** Coverage as recent as today (BusinessToday, June 15) reports the models remain offline and Anthropic is disputing the basis for the order, saying the evidence doesn't justify so broad a move. No reporting yet of a formal legal challenge or a government reversal.

I did not find any source reporting restoration or a resolution. The freshest dated item is the June 15 BusinessToday explainer; everything else clusters around June 12–13.

Sources:
- [Anthropic — Statement on the US government directive](https://www.anthropic.com/news/fable-mythos-access)
- [Anthropic on X — the directive](https://x.com/AnthropicAI/status/2065597531644743999)
- [BusinessToday — Claude Fable 5 ban explained (June 15)](https://www.businesstoday.in/technology/artificial-intelligence/story/anthropic-claude-fable-5-ban-explained-why-us-government-restricted-access-for-new-ai-models-536843-2026-06-15)
- [NBC News — Anthropic suspends new AI models after government directive](https://www.nbcnews.com/tech/tech-news/anthropic-suspends-new-ai-models-fable-mythos-government-directive-rcna349901)
- [Fortune — US bars foreigners from access](https://fortune.com/2026/06/13/anthropic-disables-fable-mythos-export-controls-national-security-threat/)
- [The New Stack — Feds order pull, three days after launch](https://thenewstack.io/us-gov-orders-anthropic-to-pull-fable-5-and-mythos-5-three-days-after-launch/)
- [BleepingComputer — Ban on foreign-national access](https://www.bleepingcomputer.com/news/security/us-gov-asks-anthropic-to-ban-foreign-national-access-to-fable-mythos/)
- [Simon Willison — documenting the cutoff](https://simonwillison.net/2026/Jun/13/us-government-directive-to-suspend-access/)
- [Octagon — When will access be restored (no timeline)](https://octagonai.co/markets/politics/when-will-anthropic-restore-fable-5-access-for-us-customers/)

A caveat on sincerity: this story is moving fast and several of these are secondary outlets paraphrasing the same Anthropic statement and Axios report — so the Lutnick/GPT-5.5/Axios specifics are as reliable as those outlets. If you need this firm, the Anthropic statement page and the AnthropicAI X post are the primary sources to trust.
